---
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import glob

TARGET_PLAYERS = {
    "Matthew Stafford": 34452,
    "Kyren Williams": 54629,
    "Puka Nacua": 56042,
}
target_nfl_ids = set(TARGET_PLAYERS.values())

# Load and filter from raw data
input_files = sorted(glob.glob("data/train/input_2023_w*.csv"))
input_dfs = [pd.read_csv(f).query("nfl_id in @target_nfl_ids") for f in input_files]
input_filtered = pd.concat(input_dfs, ignore_index=True)

relevant_plays = input_filtered[["game_id", "play_id"]].drop_duplicates()

output_files = sorted(glob.glob("data/train/output_2023_w*.csv"))
output_dfs = [
    pd.read_csv(f)
    .merge(relevant_plays, on=["game_id", "play_id"])
    .query("nfl_id in @target_nfl_ids")
    for f in output_files
]
output_filtered = pd.concat(output_dfs, ignore_index=True)

supplementary = pd.read_csv("data/supplementary_data.csv", low_memory=False)
supplementary_filtered = supplementary.merge(relevant_plays, on=["game_id", "play_id"])

# Remove plays with jitter > 1.0 (unrealistic speed oscillations)
bad_plays = set()
for name, nfl_id in TARGET_PLAYERS.items():
    player = input_filtered[input_filtered["nfl_id"] == nfl_id]
    for pid in player["play_id"].unique():
        pdata = player[player["play_id"] == pid].sort_values("frame_id")
        jitter = np.abs(np.diff(pdata["s"].values)).mean()
        if jitter > 1.0:
            game_id = pdata["game_id"].iloc[0]
            bad_plays.add((game_id, pid))
            print(
                f"  Removed: game={game_id}, play={pid} ({name}, jitter={jitter:.3f})"
            )

bad_mask = input_filtered.apply(
    lambda r: (r["game_id"], r["play_id"]) in bad_plays, axis=1
)
input_filtered = input_filtered[~bad_mask].reset_index(drop=True)
bad_mask_out = output_filtered.apply(
    lambda r: (r["game_id"], r["play_id"]) in bad_plays, axis=1
)
output_filtered = output_filtered[~bad_mask_out].reset_index(drop=True)
bad_mask_supp = supplementary_filtered.apply(
    lambda r: (r["game_id"], r["play_id"]) in bad_plays, axis=1
)
supplementary_filtered = supplementary_filtered[~bad_mask_supp].reset_index(drop=True)

print(f"\nRemoved {len(bad_plays)} plays. After cleaning:")
for name, nfl_id in TARGET_PLAYERS.items():
    pi = input_filtered[input_filtered["nfl_id"] == nfl_id]
    print(f"  {name}: {len(pi)} frames, {pi['play_id'].nunique()} plays")
print(
    f"  Total: {len(input_filtered)} input, {len(output_filtered)} output, {len(supplementary_filtered)} supplementary"
)
```

```{python}
import os

os.makedirs("data/clean", exist_ok=True)

PLAYER_FILES = {
    34452: "stafford_speed.csv",
    54629: "williams_speed.csv",
    56042: "nacua_speed.csv",
}

for nfl_id, filename in PLAYER_FILES.items():
    player = input_filtered[input_filtered["nfl_id"] == nfl_id].copy()
    player = player.sort_values(["game_id", "play_id", "frame_id"])
    player["time_s"] = np.round((player["frame_id"] - 1) * 0.1, 2)

    out = player[["game_id", "play_id", "time_s", "s"]].rename(
        columns={"s": "speed_yards_per_s"}
    )
    out.to_csv(f"data/clean/{filename}", index=False)
    print(f"  data/clean/{filename}: {len(out)} rows, {out['play_id'].nunique()} plays")

supplementary_filtered.to_csv("data/clean/supplementary.csv", index=False)
print(f"  data/clean/supplementary.csv: {len(supplementary_filtered)} rows")
```

```{python}
# Verify: load from saved clean CSVs and plot
clean_files = {
    "Matthew Stafford (QB)": "data/clean/stafford_speed.csv",
    "Kyren Williams (RB)": "data/clean/williams_speed.csv",
    "Puka Nacua (WR)": "data/clean/nacua_speed.csv",
}

fig, axes = plt.subplots(3, 1, figsize=(16, 12))
for i, (label, path) in enumerate(clean_files.items()):
    df = pd.read_csv(path)
    n_plays = df["play_id"].nunique()
    for _, pdata in df.groupby(["game_id", "play_id"]):
        axes[i].plot(
            pdata["time_s"],
            pdata["speed_yards_per_s"],
            color="steelblue",
            alpha=0.15,
            linewidth=0.5,
        )
    axes[i].set_xlabel("Time (s)")
    axes[i].set_ylabel("Speed (yards/s)")
    axes[i].set_title(f"{label} — {n_plays} plays")
    axes[i].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

for label, path in clean_files.items():
    df = pd.read_csv(path)
    print(
        f"{label}: {df['play_id'].nunique()} plays, {len(df)} frames, "
        f"speed {df['speed_yards_per_s'].min():.2f}–{df['speed_yards_per_s'].max():.2f} yards/s"
    )
```

```{python}
# Alle Spielzüge von Kyren Williams als zusammenhängendes Signal plotten
# Grün = Grenze zwischen Spielzügen, Rot = Grenze zwischen Spielen (Games)

williams_df = (
    pd.read_csv("data/clean/williams_speed.csv")
    .sort_values(["game_id", "play_id", "time_s"])
    .reset_index(drop=True)
)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(18, 4), constrained_layout=True)

x_cursor = 0
play_boundaries = []
game_boundaries = []

for game_id, game_df in williams_df.groupby("game_id", sort=True):
    for play_id, play_df in game_df.groupby("play_id", sort=True):
        speed = play_df["speed_yards_per_s"].to_numpy()
        x = np.arange(x_cursor, x_cursor + len(speed))
        ax.plot(x, speed, color="steelblue", linewidth=0.9, alpha=0.9)

        x_cursor += len(speed)
        play_boundaries.append(x_cursor - 0.5)

    game_boundaries.append(x_cursor - 0.5)

# Letzte Grenze entfernen (kein Separator nach dem letzten Segment)
if play_boundaries:
    play_boundaries = play_boundaries[:-1]
if game_boundaries:
    game_boundaries = game_boundaries[:-1]

for i, xb in enumerate(play_boundaries):
    ax.axvline(
        xb,
        color="green",
        linewidth=0.8,
        alpha=0.3,
        label="Spielzug-Grenze" if i == 0 else None,
    )

for i, xb in enumerate(game_boundaries):
    ax.axvline(
        xb,
        color="red",
        linewidth=1.8,
        alpha=0.9,
        label="Spiel-Grenze" if i == 0 else None,
    )

n_games = williams_df["game_id"].nunique()
n_plays = williams_df["play_id"].nunique()

ax.set_title(f"Kyren Williams — {n_plays} Spielzüge über {n_games} Spiele")
ax.set_xlabel("Konkatenierte Frames (10 Hz)")
ax.set_ylabel("Speed (yards/s)")
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.25)
ax.legend(loc="upper right")

plt.show()
```

```{python}
# Zoom-Plot für das 1. Spiel als eigene Figure
first_game_id = williams_df["game_id"].iloc[0]
first_game_df = williams_df[williams_df["game_id"] == first_game_id].copy()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(18, 4), constrained_layout=True)

x_cursor = 0
boundaries = []

for play_id, play_df in first_game_df.groupby("play_id", sort=True):
    speed = play_df["speed_yards_per_s"].to_numpy()
    x = np.arange(x_cursor, x_cursor + len(speed))
    ax.plot(x, speed, color="steelblue", linewidth=0.9, alpha=0.9)

    x_cursor += len(speed)
    boundaries.append(x_cursor - 0.5)

# letzte Grenze nicht zeichnen
for xb in boundaries[:-1]:
    ax.axvline(xb, color="green", linewidth=0.8, alpha=0.3)

ax.set_title(f"Kyren Williams — 1. Spiel (game_id={first_game_id})")
ax.set_xlabel("Konkatenierte Frames (10 Hz)")
ax.set_ylabel("Speed (yards/s)")
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.25)

if x_cursor > 0:
    ax.set_xlim(-0.5, x_cursor - 0.5)

plt.show()
```
